problem_statement: Given two distinct words startWord and targetWord, and a list denoting wordList of unique words of equal lengths. Find all shortest transformation sequence(s) from startWord to targetWord. You can return them in any order possible.

examples.output: [ [ 芒聙聹der芒聙聺, 芒聙聹dfr芒聙聺, 芒聙聹dfs芒聙聺 ], [ 芒聙聹der芒聙聺, 芒聙聹des芒聙聺, 芒聙聹dfs芒聙聺] ]
examples.explanation:
The length of the smallest transformation sequence here is 3.
Following are the only two shortest ways to get to the targetWord from the startWord :
"der" -> ( replace 芒聙聵r芒聙聶 by 芒聙聵s芒聙聶 ) -> "des" -> ( replace 芒聙聵e芒聙聶 by 芒聙聵f芒聙聶 ) -> "dfs".
"der" -> ( replace 芒聙聵e芒聙聶 by 芒聙聵f芒聙聶 ) -> "dfr" -> ( replace 芒聙聵r芒聙聶 by 芒聙聵s芒聙聶 ) -> "dfs".

Example 2:

examples.output: [ [ 芒聙聹gedk芒聙聺, 芒聙聹geek芒聙聺 ] ]
examples.explanation:
The length of the smallest transformation sequence here is 2.
Following is the only shortest way to get to the targetWord from the startWord :
"gedk" -> ( replace 芒聙聵d芒聙聶 by 芒聙聵e芒聙聶 ) -> "geek".
